title: Chowalloor Siva Temple
tagline: Chowalloor Siva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Chowalloor Siva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva at Guruvayoor of Thrissur District in Kerala state in India.
overview.description: Chowalloor Siva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva at Guruvayoor of Thrissur District in Kerala state in India. The presiding deity of the temple is Shiva, located in main sanctum sanatorium, facing West. According to folklore, sage Parashurama has installed the idol. The temple is a part of the 108 famous Shiva temples in Kerala and one among the five Shiva temples around Guruvayoor.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kandanissery
topPlaces.description: Kandanissery is a small village in the Indian state of Kerala. Guruvayur, a well known Hindu pilgrimage centre, is very near to Kandanissery.

topPlaces.name: Ariyannur Umbrellas
topPlaces.description: Ariyannur Umbrellas is a prehistoric Megalith burial site situated in Ariyannur in Kandanassery Panchayat of Thrissur District of Kerala. Archaeological Survey of India in 1951 declared it as a centrally protected monument.

topPlaces.name: Kandanassery
topPlaces.description: Kandanassery is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India. Located between Guruvayoor and Choondal road, Kandanassery is a village known for its coconut tappers and agriculturalists.

topPlaces.name: Kanipayyur
topPlaces.description: Kanippayyur is a village, near Kunnamkulam in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India. Kanippayyur is prominent in the practice of Traditional Architecture, Temple Building, Astrology and related studies.

topPlaces.name: Thaikkad
topPlaces.description: Thaikkad is a census town in Thrissur district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Iringaprom
topPlaces.description: Iringaprom is a census town in Thrissur district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Choondal
topPlaces.description: Choondal is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Thalapilly
topPlaces.description: Thalapilly is one of the Taluks in Thrissur District of Kerala state, south India. Before AD 1860 the headquarters of the taluk was in Kunnamkulam.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chowalloor Siva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chowalloor Siva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chowalloor Siva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (60 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (74 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chowalloor Siva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chowalloor Siva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chowalloor Siva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Chowalloor Siva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chowalloor Siva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kandanissery, Ariyannur Umbrellas, Kandanassery, Kanipayyur, Thaikkad.
